articles.title: Elon Musk Wants to Build an A.I. Satellite Factory on the Moon
articles.abstract: In a meeting with employees at his company xAI, Mr. Musk revealed a vision for a facility that includes a giant catapult to launch his satellites into space.
articles.byline: By Kate Conger and Ryan Mac

multimedia.caption: Elon Musk told employees of xAI that he plans to put a factory on the moon as a steppingstone to colonizing Mars.
multimedia.copyright: Eric Lee/The New York Times

articles.title: YouTube Argues It Isn’t Social Media in Landmark Tech Addiction Trial
articles.abstract: The app said in opening statements that it was more of an entertainment platform. The lawsuit claims social media companies design products that cause personal injury.
articles.byline: By Eli Tan and Cecilia Kang

multimedia.caption: Neal Mohan, left, the chief executive of YouTube, in 2024. The company’s lawyers argued in court on Tuesday that YouTube was an entertainment platform, not a social media platform.
multimedia.copyright: Pete Marovich for The New York Times

articles.title: A.I. Is Giving You a Personalized Internet, but You Have No Say in It
articles.abstract: The relentless addition of artificial intelligence in popular apps raises questions about what’s at stake. The answer: the future of the internet and its lifeblood, digital advertising.
articles.byline: By Brian X. Chen

articles.title: Washington Post C.E.O. Will Lewis Steps Down After Stormy Tenure
articles.abstract: His departure came days after the company cut 30 percent of the staff. He will be replaced in the interim by Jeff D’Onofrio, the chief financial officer, the company said.
articles.byline: By Benjamin Mullin, Katie Robertson and Erik Wemple

multimedia.caption: Will Lewis, the chief executive and publisher of The Washington Post, has stepped down, the company announced Saturday.

articles.title: Elon Musk Is Betting Another Tech Conglomerate (His) Can Win Over Wall St.
articles.abstract: The billionaire’s decision to merge his A.I. start-up with his rocket company will test investors’ interest in giant combinations of unalike businesses.
articles.byline: By Kate Conger and Jack Ewing

multimedia.caption: SpaceX’s launchpad near Brownsville, Texas. In addition to rockets and satellites, Elon Musk’s company now includes artificial intelligence and social media businesses.

articles.abstract: The company reported a strong holiday quarter on Thursday. But its spending, like that at other big technology companies, is starting to make investors nervous.

articles.title: Crypto Takes a Deep Slide Despite Trump’s Support
articles.abstract: The price of Bitcoin is now lower than when President Trump was elected in 2024, raising concerns of a new “crypto winter" in the industry.

articles.title: Chaos in Minneapolis Exposes an Internet at War With Truth

articles.title: A Social Network for A.I. Bots Only. No Humans Allowed.
articles.abstract: A new website called Moltbook has become the talk of Silicon Valley and a Rorschach test for belief in the state of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: How ICE Already Knows Who Minneapolis Protesters Are
articles.abstract: Agents use facial recognition, social media monitoring and other tech tools not only to identify undocumented immigrants but also to track protesters, current and former officials said.
articles.byline: By Sheera Frenkel and Aaron Krolik

articles.title: Apple Reports Record iPhone Sales Amid Holiday Bump
articles.abstract: New phone designs, coupled with robust holiday spending, continued to lift iPhone sales in the quarter and drove the company to record profits.
articles.byline: By Kalley Huang

articles.title: How the A.I. Boom Could Push Up the Price of Your Next PC
articles.abstract: A.I. companies are buying up memory chips, causing the prices of those components — which are also used in laptops and smartphones — to soar.
articles.byline: By Don Clark and Loren Elliott

articles.title: Amazon and Google Eat Into Nvidia’s A.I. Chip Supremacy
articles.abstract: The rivals made billions of dollars in the business over the past year, showing other companies that Nvidia isn’t the only game in town.
